title: Aspirality and separability for surface subgroups in 3-manifold groups
speaker: Yi Liu

proof_stages.label: Separability as virtual embeddedness
proof_stages.summary: The talk reformulates separability of a surface subgroup as the existence of a finite cover in which the corresponding pi_1-injective immersed surface lifts, up to homotopy, to an embedded surface, and relates this to the virtual Haken strategy.

proof_stages.label: Geometric pieces have no separability obstruction
proof_stages.summary: For irreducible geometric pieces, Seifert-fibered surfaces split into vertical and horizontal types while hyperbolic ones split into geometrically finite and virtual-fiber types, leading to the conclusion that pi_1-injective immersed surfaces in purely geometric 3-manifolds are virtually embedded.

proof_stages.label: JSJ gluing and the almost-fiber reduction
proof_stages.summary: In a non-geometric manifold the local virtual embeddedness of surface pieces must be matched across JSJ tori, so the speaker introduces dual decomposition graphs, the induced surface decomposition, and the almost-fiber part Phi_s, then states that virtual embeddedness of S is equivalent to virtual embeddedness of Phi_s, with the remaining pieces handled using Przytycki-Wise type results.

proof_stages.label: Graph-manifold spirality obstruction
proof_stages.summary: Using the Rubinstein-Wang graph-manifold setting of horizontally immersed surfaces, the obstruction is described by comparing levels in the surface-subgroup cover and then encoded as a spirality homomorphism from cycles in the almost-fiber part to nonzero rationals, computable as products of intersection-number ratios with adjacent Seifert fibers.

proof_stages.label: Extension of spirality to mixed and hyperbolic pieces
proof_stages.summary: To replace the missing Seifert fiber directions in hyperbolic pieces, the construction uses suspension flows and slopes of degeneracy in mapping-torus pieces, then reformulates the invariant via covering degrees to obtain a general criterion: the surface is virtually embedded exactly when the invariant has only values plus or minus one.

proof_stages.label: Construction of obstructed surfaces in hyperbolic-piece mapping tori
proof_stages.summary: The speaker outlines a mapping-torus construction from a doubled pseudo-Anosov map composed with a Dehn twist, then sketches how semicovers, deliberately mismatched slopes, and quasiconvex closing surfaces produce an immersed surface with nontrivial spirality and hence no virtual embedding.

examples.name: Product Seifert model F x S^1
examples.note: The product of a hyperbolic-type surface with a circle is used to make the vertical/horizontal surface dichotomy concrete, including annuli over arcs and horizontal finite-cover fibers; it illustrates already stated structure rather than generating the proof idea.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker analyzes the product Seifert model by describing vertical annuli as arcs in F crossed with fibers and horizontal surfaces as finite covers/factors.

examples.name: Cycle meeting two JSJ curves
examples.note: The drawn case of a cycle in the almost-fiber part crossing two decomposition curves is a concrete visualization of the general product formula for spirality ratios.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker uses a cycle crossing JSJ curves to define the spirality invariant via the crossed curves and intersection-number ratios.
